questions.en: It's quite absurd of you to tell her that sort of thing.

questions.q: あまり（　　）になるなよ。
questions.exp: 強気（つよき）= firm, strong　→ 「あまり強気になるなよ。」
questions.en: Don't press your luck, kid.

questions.q: あの老人たちは（　　）に無駄話をしに行くのが好きだ。
questions.exp: 街角（まちかど）= street corner　→ 「あの老人たちは街角に無駄話をしに行くのが好きだ。」
questions.en: The old men like to meet on the street corner just to shoot the bull.

questions.q: あの人はあまり（　　）から好きになれない。
questions.exp: 威張る（いばる）= to be proud, to swagger　→ 「あの人はあまり威張るから好きになれない。」
questions.en: That man is too boastful for my liking.

questions.q: あの支部は（　　）労働組合に属している。
questions.exp: 炭鉱（たんこう）= coal mine, coal pit　→ 「あの支部は炭鉱労働組合に属している。」
questions.en: That branch is affiliated to the miners' union.

questions.q: あなたは本当に幽霊を見たのではない。それは（　　）のものでしかなかったのだ。
questions.exp: 架空（かくう）= imaginary, fiction, fanciful　→ 「あなたは本当に幽霊を見たのではない。それは架空のものでしかなかったのだ。」
questions.en: You didn't really see a ghost―it was only imaginary.

questions.q: 本当に（　　）が上手ですね。
questions.exp: 裁縫（さいほう）= sewing　→ 「本当に裁縫が上手ですね。」
questions.en: You're a magician with a needle and thread.

questions.q: 作文を（　　）する必要はありません。
questions.exp: 清書（せいしょ）= clean copy　→ 「作文を清書する必要はありません。」
questions.en: You don't have to write out a clean copy of your composition.

questions.q: あなたはこの（　　）を一人で書いたのですか。
questions.exp: 童話（どうわ）= fairy tale　→ 「あなたはこの童話を一人で書いたのですか。」
questions.en: Did you write this fairy tale by yourself?

questions.q: あなたの（　　）はどうつづるのですか。
questions.exp: 名字（みょうじ）= surname, family name　→ 「あなたの名字はどうつづるのですか。」
questions.en: How do you spell your family name?

questions.q: あなたの出発の準備がすべて（　　）よう取り計らいます。
questions.exp: 整う（ととのう）= to be prepared, to be in order, to be arranged　→ 「あなたの出発の準備がすべて整うよう取り計らいます。」
questions.en: I will see to it that everything is ready for your departure.

questions.q: あなたの（　　）には私たちとしてはまったく不満だ。
questions.exp: 解答（かいとう）= answer, solution　→ 「あなたの解答には私たちとしてはまったく不満だ。」
questions.en: Your answer is anything but satisfactory to us.

questions.q: ７月の（　　）は、海に行くのは早いかな？
questions.exp: 上旬（じょうじゅん）= first 10 days of month　→ 「７月の上旬は、海に行くのは早いかな？」
questions.en: I wonder if the first week or so of July is too early to go to the beach.

questions.exp: 偶数（ぐうすう）= even number　→ 「２、４、６などは偶数です。」
questions.en: Two, four, six, etc. are even numbers.

questions.q: この工場では自動車の部品を（　　）している。
questions.exp: 製作（せいさく）= manufacture, production　→ 「この工場では自動車の部品を製作している。」
questions.en: This factory manufactures automobile parts.

questions.q: 私は（　　）効果がすばらしいので「ターミネーター」が好きです。
questions.exp: 特殊（とくしゅ）= special, unique　→ 「私は特殊効果がすばらしいので「ターミネーター」が好きです。」
questions.en: I like the Terminator films because the special effects are fantastic.

questions.q: 雪の多いところでは、（　　）は必需品です。
questions.exp: 灯油（とうゆ）= lamp oil, kerosene　→ 「雪の多いところでは、灯油は必需品です。」
questions.en: In places where it snows a lot, kerosene is an indispensable item.

questions.q: アナグマは深い穴を（　　）。
questions.exp: 掘る（ほる）= to dig, to excavate　→ 「アナグマは深い穴を掘る。」
questions.en: Badgers dig deep holes.

questions.q: 日本の（　　）風景は大きく変わったといわれています。
questions.exp: 農村（のうそん）= agricultural community　→ 「日本の農村風景は大きく変わったといわれています。」
questions.en: It's said that Japan's rural landscape has changed greatly.

questions.q: 昭和１０年は（　　）１９３５年です。
questions.exp: 西暦（せいれき）= Christian Era, after (Christ’s) death (A.D.)　→ 「昭和１０年は西暦１９３５年です。」
questions.en: The 10th year of Showa is 1935 in the Western calendar.

questions.q: 土地は８つに（　　）された。
questions.exp: 区分（くぶん）= division, section, classification　→ 「土地は８つに区分された。」
questions.en: The land was divided into 8 lots.

questions.q: 新年（　　）、ご挨拶申し上げます。
questions.exp: 〜にあたって：on the occasion of / at the time of (formal)。〜にあたって marks an important occasion, milestone, or starting point at which the main-clause action is taken. It is formal
questions.en: On the occasion of the new year, I offer my greetings.

questions.q: 会議（　　）、資料を配布した。
questions.exp: 〜に先立って：prior to / in advance of。〜に先立って marks an action taken BEFORE an important upcoming event — 'prior to X, Y'. It is formal and usually appears in b
questions.en: Prior to the meeting, I distributed the materials.

questions.q: 事故（　　）、安全対策を強化した。
questions.exp: 〜を契機に：taking X as the opportunity / prompted by。〜を契機に is a more formal / literary variant of 〜をきっかけに, marking an opportunity or triggering event that led to a significa
questions.en: Prompted by the accident, safety measures were strengthened.

questions.q: 本日は定休日（　　）、閉店いたします。
questions.exp: 〜につき：due to / because of / per。〜につき has two related senses: (1) 'due to / because of' — giving a formal reason, common in signage ('closed due to renov
questions.en: As today is our regular closing day, we are closed.

questions.q: 彼は正直である（　　）、損をすることも多い。
questions.exp: 〜がゆえに：because / therefore (literary)。〜ゆえに is a literary / archaic causal marker meaning 'because X, therefore Y'. It is used in essays, formal writing, legal
questions.en: Precisely because he is honest, he often loses out.

questions.q: 桜の木が多い（　　）、この町は'桜町'と呼ばれている。
questions.exp: 〜ことから：from the fact that / based on the fact。〜ことから presents a fact or evidence as the basis for a conclusion, explanation, or designation — 'from the fact that X, (Y
questions.en: From the fact that it has many cherry trees, this town is called 'Sakura Town'.

questions.q: 驚き（　　）、言葉を失った。
questions.exp: 〜のあまり：out of excess / from too much。〜あまり expresses an excessive degree of emotion, sensation, or state that led to the main-clause consequence — 'out of too
questions.en: Out of sheer surprise, I was lost for words.

questions.q: 君を愛していれ（　　）、こう言うのだ。
questions.exp: 〜ばこそ：precisely because / it is only because。〜ばこそ emphasizes the X clause as the sole or essential reason for Y — 'precisely because X, Y'. Formal / literary. Often
questions.en: It's precisely because I love you that I say this.

questions.q: 大切な友達だ（　　）、厳しいことも言う。
questions.exp: 〜からこそ：precisely because / it is exactly because。〜からこそ emphasizes that the X clause is the essential and exclusive reason for Y — 'precisely because X, Y (and no other r
questions.en: It's precisely because you're a dear friend that I say harsh things too.

questions.q: 少子化（　　）、学校が減っている。
questions.exp: 〜に伴って：accompanying / along with / as X progresses。〜に伴って expresses that one change or event accompanies another — 'along with X, Y also (happens / changes)'. Similar to 〜に
questions.en: Along with the declining birthrate, the number of schools is decreasing.

questions.q: 能力（　　）、仕事が与えられる。
questions.exp: 〜に応じて：depending on / in response to / in accordance with。〜に応じて expresses proportional or responsive adjustment — the main clause action or outcome varies 'depending on' or 'in a
questions.en: Work is given according to ability.

questions.q: 観客の声援（　　）、選手たちはベストを尽くした。
questions.exp: 〜にこたえて：in response to / responding to (expectations, requests)。〜にこたえて expresses that the main-clause action responds to or meets an expectation, request, demand, or cheer from the X n
questions.en: Responding to the audience's cheers, the athletes gave their best.

questions.q: 年齢（　　）、誰でも参加できます。
questions.exp: 〜にかかわらず：regardless of / irrespective of。〜にかかわらず expresses that the main clause holds regardless of variation in the X factor — 'regardless of X, Y'. Often used
questions.en: Regardless of age, anyone can participate.

questions.q: 悪天候（　　）、試合は行われた。
questions.exp: 〜にもかかわらず：despite / in spite of / even though。〜にもかかわらず is a formal concessive marker meaning 'despite X, Y'. More formal than のに or が, with no emotional connotation —
questions.en: Despite the bad weather, the match was held.

questions.q: 男女（　　）、応募できます。
questions.exp: 〜を問わず：regardless of / without regard to。〜を問わず expresses that a main-clause condition or rule applies regardless of variation in the X factor — 'regardless of X'
questions.en: Regardless of gender, you can apply.

questions.q: 冗談（　　）、話を戻しましょう。
questions.exp: 〜はともかくとして：setting X aside / putting X aside / regardless of。〜はともかく sets aside a topic or factor as not worth discussing right now, to focus on something else — 'setting X aside, le
questions.en: Joking aside, let's get back to the topic.

questions.q: 冗談（　　）、真面目な話をしましょう。
questions.exp: 〜はさておき：setting X aside (formal)。〜はさておき is a more formal variant of 〜はともかく, used to set a topic aside and move to another. Common in written Japanese, es
questions.en: Setting jokes aside, let's have a serious conversation.

questions.q: 急いでいる時（　　）、電車が遅れる。
questions.exp: 〜に限って：of all (things/people/times) / precisely when。〜に限って has two related senses: (1) 'of all X' — emphasizing that X is the exception or the one that (unexpectedly) did Y;
questions.en: Of all times, the train gets delayed when I'm in a hurry.

questions.q: 日本（　　）、世界中でこの問題がある。
questions.exp: 〜に限らず：not limited to / not only X (but also)。〜に限らず expresses that the main-clause statement applies not only to X but also to other, related cases — 'not limited to
questions.en: Not only in Japan — this problem exists around the world.

questions.q: 彼の優しさに、感動せ（　　）。
questions.exp: 〜ずにはいられない：can't help doing / can't stop oneself from。〜ずにはいられない is a strong involuntary expression — 'can't help but do X'. The speaker feels compelled to act due to strong e
questions.en: I can't help being moved by his kindness.

questions.q: こうし（　　）。早く行こう。
questions.exp: 〜てはいられない：can't afford to be (doing X) / can't stay (in state X)。〜てはいられない expresses that the speaker cannot afford to remain in a state or continue doing an action due to urgency or pre
questions.en: I can't just stay like this. Let's hurry.

questions.q: 失敗する（　　）、挑戦するべきだ。
questions.exp: 〜にせよ：even if / whether or not (formal)。〜にしろ / 〜にせよ are formal literary variants of 〜にしても ('even if'). They can appear singly as a concessive, or in paired form
questions.en: Even if you fail, you should try.

questions.q: 失敗した（　　）、経験にはなる。
questions.exp: 〜としても：even if / even assuming / even as。〜としても is a concessive marker meaning 'even if X', 'even assuming X'. It hypothetically accepts X and then states somethi
questions.en: Even if I fail, it will become experience.

questions.q: 夢を実現す（　　）、毎日努力している。
questions.exp: 〜べく：in order to / for the purpose of (literary)。〜べく is a literary purpose marker meaning 'in order to X'. It is derived from the classical form that also gives 〜べきだ ('s
questions.en: In order to realize my dream, I work hard every day.

questions.q: 子供（　　）、大人も楽しめる。
questions.exp: 〜はもちろんのこと：not to mention / of course / needless to say。〜はもちろん presents X as an expected or given case and the main clause adds an additional, sometimes more surprising, case —
questions.en: Needless to say children, but adults can also enjoy it.

questions.q: 友達（　　）彼に紹介された。
questions.exp: 〜を通じて：through / via / throughout (all of)。〜を通じて / 〜を通して has two related senses: (1) 'through / via' — by means of something (intermediary, medium, experience); (2
questions.en: I was introduced to him through a friend.

questions.q: 10年（　　）研究を続けた。
questions.exp: 〜にわたって：over / spanning / across (a period or extent)。〜にわたって expresses that the main-clause action or state spans a period, area, or extent — 'over / throughout X'. Formal re
questions.en: I continued the research over 10 years.

questions.q: 愛（　　）手紙を書いた。
questions.exp: 〜を込めて：with (feeling) / putting (feeling) into。〜を込めて expresses doing an action with a particular feeling or emotion 'put into' it — 'with love', 'with care', 'with gra
questions.en: I wrote the letter with love.

questions.q: 初めて（　　）、上手に弾けた。
questions.exp: 〜にしては：for (a) / considering X (surprising)。〜にしては expresses that the main-clause observation is surprising considering the X premise — 'for an X, (Y is unexpected)'
questions.en: For a first try, I played it well.

questions.q: 彼は80歳（　　）、今も元気だ。
questions.exp: 〜にして：at (the point of) / being (X), (emphatic)。〜にして has several related literary senses: (1) at a specific (often emphatic) point — '80歳にして' ('at 80'); (2) 'being X (s
questions.en: At 80 years old, he is still energetic.

questions.q: 長年練習した（　　）。演奏が素晴らしい。
questions.exp: 〜だけのことはある：(it's) as expected from / lives up to。〜だけのことはある expresses that the main-clause praise or quality is fitting given a stated premise — 'being X, it's no surpris
questions.en: The years of practice have paid off. The performance is wonderful.

questions.q: そんなことで病院に行く（　　）。
questions.exp: 〜までもない：no need to even / not worth (doing)。〜までもない expresses that an action is so unnecessary or obvious that it isn't even worth doing — 'no need to go so far as X
questions.en: There's no need to go so far as to visit a hospital for that.

questions.q: 困っている人を助けるのが友情（　　）。
questions.exp: 〜というものだ：that's what X is / that's (the nature of) X。〜というものだ expresses a speaker's evaluative conclusion or universal statement about what something is or should be — 'that'
questions.en: Helping a friend in need — that's what friendship is.

questions.q: 急いでいた（　　）、挨拶もせずに帰ってしまった。
questions.exp: 〜ものだから：because / it's because (excusing / justifying)。〜ものだから gives a reason with an excuse-making or justifying flavor — 'it's because X (so please understand)'. Often used w
questions.en: Because I was in a hurry, I left without even saying goodbye.

questions.q: あの人は話し方（　　）失礼だ。
questions.exp: 〜からして：judging from / starting with (as a representative example)。〜からして has two related senses: (1) 'judging from X' — where X is given as a basis for the main-clause judgment; (2) 'star
questions.en: That person is rude — just from the way they speak, you can tell.

questions.q: 専門家（　　）、この問題は簡単だ。
questions.exp: 〜からすると：from X's standpoint / judging from。〜からすると / 〜からすれば expresses that the main clause is judged from the perspective of X — 'from X's standpoint' or 'judging f
questions.en: From an expert's standpoint, this problem is easy.

questions.q: 約束した（　　）、必ず守ります。
questions.exp: 〜からには：now that / since (with strong obligation)。〜からには expresses a strong commitment based on a premise — 'now that X, one must Y'. The main clause typically expresses o
questions.en: Now that I've promised, I will definitely keep it.

questions.q: 彼は頭がいい（　　）、性格もいい。
questions.exp: 〜のみならず：not only X but also Y (literary)。〜のみならず is a literary 'not only' construction — the most formal variant of the 'not only' family (〜だけでなく, 〜ばかりでなく, 〜のみならず
questions.en: Not only is he smart, but he also has a good personality.

questions.q: もう春（　　）、まだ寒い。
questions.exp: 〜とはいえ：although / that said / even so。〜とはいえ is a formal concessive meaning 'although X' or 'that said, X'. It hedges a statement by noting that while X is tru
questions.en: Although it's already spring, it's still cold.

questions.q: 春だ（　　）、今朝は雪が降った。
questions.exp: 〜とはいうものの：that said / although one says。〜とはいうものの is a literary concessive similar to 〜とはいえ — 'although one says X, (the reality is Y)'. Often used to qualify a
questions.en: Although we say it's spring, snow fell this morning.

questions.q: 子供（　　）、当然の反応だ。
questions.exp: 〜にしてみれば：from X's perspective / from X's point of view。〜にしてみれば / 〜にしてみたら expresses the main-clause observation from the perspective of a specific person or group — 'from X's p
questions.en: From a child's perspective, it's a natural reaction.

questions.q: お金がある（　　）、幸せとは限らない。
questions.exp: 〜からといって：just because / even though (one says)。〜からといって rejects a simplistic causal inference — 'just because X (doesn't mean Y)'. The main clause often uses a negative
questions.en: Just because you have money doesn't necessarily mean you're happy.

questions.q: 時間に厳しい田中さん（　　）、きっと時間通りに来るだろう。
questions.exp: 〜のことだから：knowing X / given that it's X。〜のことだから expresses that the speaker is making a prediction based on knowledge of X's character or habits — 'knowing X, (t
questions.en: Knowing Mr. Tanaka, who is strict about time, he'll surely come on time.

questions.q: 日本に来（　　）、毎日が楽しい。
questions.exp: 〜てからというもの：ever since / since that time。〜てからというもの emphasizes a dramatic change or ongoing state that began from a specific past event — 'ever since X, Y has con
questions.en: Ever since I came to Japan, every day has been enjoyable.

questions.q: 雨だけど、雪じゃない（　　）。
questions.exp: 〜だけましだ：at least X (is better than nothing) / could be worse。〜だけましだ expresses a minor consolation — 'at least X is the case, and that's better than worse alternatives'. Used when fi
questions.en: It's raining, but at least it's not snowing.

questions.q: 彼は家に着く（　　）、ベッドに倒れ込んだ。
questions.exp: 〜や否や：as soon as / the moment that (literary)。〜や否や is a literary / formal construction meaning 'as soon as X, Y'. The main clause event follows immediately and withou
questions.en: As soon as he got home, he collapsed into bed.

questions.q: 家に着くか着（　　）、雨が降り出した。
questions.exp: 〜かないかのうちに：no sooner (had X) than Y / barely had one Xed when Y。〜か〜ないかのうちに expresses that the main-clause event happened almost simultaneously with or even before the X event completed
questions.en: No sooner had I arrived home than it started to rain.

questions.q: 教え（　　）忘れてしまう。
questions.exp: 〜たそばから：as soon as / immediately after (with repeated undoing)。〜そばから expresses 'no sooner had X happened than Y undid it' — a repetitive cycle where an action is immediately negated o
questions.en: He forgets as soon as I teach him.

questions.q: 子供（　　）、そんなことで泣かないで。
questions.exp: 〜じゃあるまいし：it's not as if / since it's not like。〜じゃあるまいし expresses 'it's not as if X, so (you should act accordingly)' — a construction that rejects a childish or unrea
questions.en: You're not a child — don't cry over that.

questions.q: 彼は「もう限界だ」（　　）、ため息をついた。
questions.exp: 〜とばかりに：as if to say / seemingly saying。〜とばかりに expresses that someone's action or behavior seems to convey a specific message, without them actually saying it —
questions.en: He sighed as if to say 'I've had enough'.

questions.q: あなたのため（　　）、何でもする。
questions.exp: 〜とあれば：if it's (a case of) / if it's for。〜とあれば expresses a hypothetical or conditional framing — 'if it is the case of X' / 'if it's for X'. Often carries a sens
questions.en: If it's for you, I'll do anything.

questions.q: 完璧とは言わ（　　）、かなりよくできている。
questions.exp: 〜ないまでも：even if not quite X / not necessarily X, but at least。〜ないまでも expresses 'even if not quite X, at least Y' — the speaker concedes that the full X is not achieved, but Y, a less
questions.en: I wouldn't call it perfect, but it's done quite well.

questions.q: 有名な先生（　　）、ピアノを学んだ。
questions.exp: 〜のもとで：under (the guidance / condition / auspices of)。〜のもとで / 〜のもとに expresses that an action takes place under the supervision, guidance, conditions, or influence of X — 'und
questions.en: I studied piano under a famous teacher.

questions.q: 今日は終わり（　　）。
questions.exp: 〜そうもない：doesn't look like / there's no sign of。〜そうにない / 〜そうもない is the negative of the appearance-based そうだ — 'doesn't look like X will happen'. Common with future pred
questions.en: It doesn't look like I'll finish today.

questions.q: 忙しくて、彼に会え（　　）だった。
questions.exp: 〜ずじまい：ended up not (doing) / didn't manage to。〜ずじまい expresses regret that an intended action was not carried out — 'ended up not X-ing'. Common in retrospectives: 'I
questions.en: I was so busy I didn't manage to see him.

questions.q: 今さら謝っ（　　）、許してもらえない。
questions.exp: 〜たところで：even if / no matter if (futile effort)。〜たところで expresses that even if X is done, the main-clause outcome will not change — 'even if you do X, (Y won't change /
questions.en: Even if you apologize now, you won't be forgiven.

questions.q: （　　）、彼は経験が豊富だ。
questions.exp: 〜なにしろ：after all / you see / it's because。なにしろ is a sentence-initial adverb that introduces an emphatic explanation or justification — 'after all', 'the thing is'
questions.en: After all, he has a wealth of experience.

questions.q: （　　）、久しぶりだからよく覚えていない。
questions.exp: 〜なにせ：after all / the thing is。なにせ is a sentence-initial adverb very similar in function to なにしろ — introducing an emphatic explanation. 'After all X' o
questions.en: After all, it's been a long time, so I don't remember well.

questions.q: この仕事は給料がいい（　　）、責任が重い。
questions.exp: 〜反面：on the other hand / while (one side)。〜反面 presents two contrasting aspects of the same subject — 'on the one hand X, on the other Y'. Functionally similar to
questions.en: This job has good pay, but on the other hand, heavy responsibility.

questions.q: 外国人（　　）、日本は安全な国だ。
questions.exp: 〜から見ると：from the viewpoint of / from X's perspective。〜から見ると expresses that an evaluation or observation is made from a specific vantage point — 'from X's viewpoint'. Very cl
questions.en: From a foreigner's perspective, Japan is a safe country.

questions.q: ニュースによると、明日は雨が降る（　　）。
questions.exp: 〜ということだ：I hear that / it means that。〜ということだ has two related senses: (1) hearsay / report — 'I hear that X' / 'they say X'; (2) meaning / conclusion — 'it me
questions.en: According to the news, it's going to rain tomorrow.

questions.q: 議論（　　）、結論が出た。
questions.exp: 〜の結果：as a result of / after。〜結果 (けっか) introduces the result or outcome of a process — 'as a result of X, Y'. Formal / written register, common in re
questions.en: As a result of the discussion, a conclusion was reached.

questions.q: 天気（　　）、予定を変更する。
questions.exp: 〜次第で：depending on / according to。〜次第で expresses that an outcome or action depends on the X factor — 'depending on X, (the result varies)'. Similar in mea
questions.en: I'll change the plan depending on the weather.

questions.q: 事故（　　）原因を調査した。
questions.exp: 〜に至る：reach / arrive at / come to。〜に至る expresses that a process, situation, or movement has reached or extended to X — 'came to X' / 'reached X'. Formal /
questions.en: We investigated the causes leading to the accident.

questions.q: 子供からお年寄り（　　）、みんな楽しめる。
questions.exp: 〜に至るまで：even to / all the way to / as far as。〜に至るまで expresses extension or comprehensiveness — 'even reaching X' / 'all the way to X'. Often used to emphasize the th
questions.en: Everyone, from children all the way to the elderly, can enjoy it.

questions.q: ご利用（　　）は、注意事項をお読みください。
questions.exp: 〜の際に：on the occasion of / at the time of (formal)。〜際(に) marks a significant occasion or moment when something is done — 'on the occasion of X, Y'. More formal than 〜とき an
questions.en: Upon use, please read the precautions.

questions.q: 長年の研究（　　）、ついに発見した。
questions.exp: 〜を経て：after going through / via / through (a process)。〜を経て marks a process or step that was gone through before reaching the main-clause outcome — 'after going through X'. Co
questions.en: After many years of research, we finally discovered it.

questions.q: 現状（　　）、対策を立てた。
questions.exp: 〜を踏まえて：based on / taking into account / in view of。〜を踏まえて expresses that the main-clause action or conclusion is based on or takes into account X — 'taking X into account'
questions.en: Based on the current situation, we formulated a countermeasure.

questions.q: 親の心配（　　）、彼は一人で旅に出た。
questions.exp: 〜をよそに：ignoring / in disregard of。〜をよそに expresses that the main-clause action proceeds in disregard of X — 'ignoring X', 'in spite of X'. Often carries a
questions.en: Disregarding his parents' worries, he set off alone on a trip.

questions.q: 予定（　　）に出発した。
questions.exp: 〜どおり：just as / according to / exactly like。〜通り / 〜どおり expresses that an action or outcome is exactly as X was described, predicted, or specified — 'just as X', 'ac
questions.en: We departed just as planned.

questions.q: 結果（　　）、今後の方針を決める。
questions.exp: 〜いかんで：depending on / according to (formal)。〜いかん is a formal / literary variant of 〜次第で ('depending on'). Common in business and official statements. Variants: いかんで
questions.en: Depending on the result, we'll decide future policy.

questions.q: それはあり（　　）。
questions.exp: 〜得ない：is possible / can / is capable of。〜得る (うる / える) attaches to a verb's ます-stem to indicate possibility — 'can / is possible to do X'. The dictionary form 得る
questions.en: That's impossible.

questions.q: 休日（　　）、公園は大勢の人で賑わった。
questions.exp: 〜とあって：because it's / given that it's (special situation)。〜とあって expresses that the main-clause phenomenon is occurring because of a special circumstance X — 'because it's X (a no
questions.en: Being a holiday, the park was crowded with many people.

questions.q: 説明を聞い（　　）、始められない。
questions.exp: 〜てからでないと：not until after / unless you first。〜てからでないと expresses that the main clause cannot happen until after the X action is completed — 'unless you first do X, yo
questions.en: We can't start until after hearing the explanation.

questions.q: 実際に見てみ（　　）、分からない。
questions.exp: 〜ないことには：unless / without doing (X, can't do Y)。〜ないことには expresses that the main-clause action or outcome is impossible unless X is done first — 'unless you do X, (Y is
questions.en: Unless I actually see it, I can't tell.

questions.q: 彼は忙しい（　　）。
questions.exp: 〜とみえる：it seems that / appears to。〜とみえる expresses that something appears to be the case based on observable evidence — 'it seems that X'. Similar to 〜ようだ
questions.en: It seems he is busy.

questions.q: もし雨が降る（　　）、どうしますか。
questions.exp: 〜とすれば：supposing / assuming / if it's the case that。〜とすれば (with variants 〜とすると, 〜としたら) expresses a hypothetical assumption — 'supposing that X, then Y'. The speaker accepts
questions.en: Supposing it rains, what will you do?

questions.q: 漢字（　　）、ひらがなも書けない。
questions.exp: 〜はおろか：let alone / not to mention。〜はおろか expresses that even a basic/less-demanding case X is impossible, let alone a more-demanding Y — 'not even X, let a
questions.en: Not to mention kanji, I can't even write hiragana.

questions.q: それは単なる言い訳（　　）。
questions.exp: 〜にすぎない：is nothing more than / merely。〜にすぎない expresses that something is 'nothing more than X' — a dismissive or minimizing description. Common for downplayin
questions.en: That's nothing more than a mere excuse.

questions.q: 言（　　）、聞いてくれないよ。
questions.exp: 〜ったって：even if (casual)。〜たって / 〜っ たって is a casual contraction of 〜ても ('even if'). Common in casual speech, especially when dismissing a hypothet
questions.en: Even if I told him, he wouldn't listen.

questions.q: 初めて（　　）、不手際をお許しください。
questions.exp: 〜のこととて：since / because (formal, explanatory)。〜こととて is a formal / literary reason marker — 'since X is the case, Y (please excuse)'. Often used in apologies or explan
questions.en: Since it's my first time, please forgive any mishandling.

questions.q: お客様（　　）商売です。
questions.exp: 〜あっての：only possible thanks to / made possible by。〜あっての expresses that the main-clause noun is only possible because of X — 'only possible thanks to X'. The X is presente
questions.en: It's a business that exists only thanks to our customers.

questions.q: 法律（　　）、この行為は違法だ。
questions.exp: 〜に照らして：in light of / in accordance with。〜に照らして expresses that something is judged or evaluated 'in light of' or 'in accordance with' a standard, rule, or preced
questions.en: In light of the law, this act is illegal.

questions.q: 彼が来ないのは、寂しい（　　）。
questions.exp: 〜限りだ：feel extremely / the limit of (an emotion)。〜限りだ expresses that the speaker feels something to an extreme degree — 'I'm extremely X'. Attached to an emotional adjec
questions.en: Him not coming is extremely lonely.

questions.q: 彼の態度は失礼（　　）。
questions.exp: 〜極まりない：extremely / in the extreme。〜極まりない and 〜極まる both express extreme degree — 'extremely X' — attached to an emotional or evaluative na-adjective. Liter
questions.en: His attitude is extremely rude.

questions.q: 言わ（　　）分かるだろう。
questions.exp: 〜ずとも：even without / even if not。〜ずとも is a formal / literary concessive meaning 'even without doing X' or 'even if not X'. Combines the formal negative ず
questions.en: You should understand without me saying it.

questions.q: 専門家は今後の見通しを楽観的（　　）。
questions.exp: 〜とみる：see as / regard as / judge to be。〜とみる expresses that the speaker considers or judges X to be the case — 'I see it as X' / 'I regard it as X'. Formal / an
questions.en: Experts see the future outlook as optimistic.

questions.q: 彼は強い（　　）、実は優しい。
questions.exp: 〜ようでいて：seemingly X, but actually / looks X but。〜ようで expresses apparent contradiction — 'seemingly X, but actually Y'. The surface appearance X contrasts with the deepe
questions.en: He seems strong, but is actually gentle.

questions.q: お金（　　）あれば、何でも買える。
questions.exp: 〜さえ：if only / as long as (the minimal condition is met)。〜さえ〜ば / 〜さえすれば expresses a sufficient (often minimal) condition for the main clause to hold — 'as long as X, Y' / 'if on
questions.en: As long as you have money, you can buy anything.

questions.q: （　　）、嫌なんだもん。
questions.exp: 〜だって：because / but / however (casual defensive)。だって at the beginning of a sentence is a casual way to give a reason or excuse — 'because', 'but'. Often carries a defens
questions.en: But I just don't want to!

questions.q: （　　）問題はありません。
questions.exp: 〜これといって：nothing in particular / nothing notable。〜といって (especially in the fixed form これといって) expresses that there is 'nothing in particular' worth noting — 'no specific
questions.en: There's nothing in particular that's a problem.

questions.q: そんなに怠ける（　　）、合格できないよ。
questions.exp: 〜ようでは：if it's like that / at this rate。〜ようでは expresses a hypothetical condition with a negative, critical, or warning tone — 'if X is like that, (then somethin
questions.en: If you're lazy like that, you won't pass.

questions.q: 彼女はいつも黒（　　）の服を着ている。
questions.exp: 〜ずくめ：nothing but / entirely / all X。〜ずくめ expresses that something is entirely or exclusively X — 'nothing but X', 'all X'. Often used for qualities, colors,
questions.en: She is always dressed all in black.

questions.q: 子供は泥（　　）になって遊んでいた。
questions.exp: 〜まみれ：smeared / covered with (often messy or unpleasant)。〜まみれ expresses that something is physically covered or smeared with X, typically an unpleasant or messy substance — mud,
questions.en: The child was playing, covered in mud.

questions.q: いい子（　　）のはやめなさい。
questions.exp: 〜ぶる：pretend to be / put on airs of。〜ぶる creates a verb meaning 'pretend to be X' or 'put on airs of being X'. Often carries a mildly critical nuance — the s
questions.en: Stop pretending to be a good kid.

questions.q: 彼の演奏には感動的な（　　）。
questions.exp: 〜ものがある：there is something (about it) / is impressively。〜ものがある expresses that there is a certain quality or feature worth noting about something — 'there's something X about it
questions.en: There's something moving about his performance.

questions.q: 彼は優しい（　　）、おとなしいタイプだ。
questions.exp: 〜というか：or rather / I mean。〜というか is a discourse marker used when the speaker is searching for a more accurate description or hedging a previous sta
questions.en: He's kind, or rather, a quiet type.

questions.q: 見る（　　）窓の外を眺めていた。
questions.exp: 〜ともなく：unintentionally / without consciously。〜ともなく expresses that an action is performed unintentionally, without conscious focus — 'unintentionally X' / 'without me
questions.en: I was gazing out the window without really looking.

questions.q: 結果（　　）、大成功だった。
questions.exp: 〜からいうと：from X's viewpoint / speaking from X。〜からいうと / 〜からいえば / 〜からいって is a variant of 〜からすると that expresses perspective — 'from X's viewpoint'. Common in analysis an
questions.en: From the results, it was a big success.

questions.q: 行きたくなく（　　）が、時間がない。
questions.exp: 〜もない：not that I don't / it's not that X isn't。〜なくもない is a double negative that hedges a positive statement — 'it's not that I don't X' / 'it's not impossible that X'.
questions.en: It's not that I don't want to go, but I don't have time.

questions.q: 成功はあなたの努力（　　）。
questions.exp: 〜にかかっている：depends on / hinges on。〜にかかっている expresses that something 'depends on' or 'hinges on' X — the outcome is determined by X. Common when emphasizin
questions.en: Success depends on your effort.

questions.q: 友情（　　）、お金で買えないものだ。
questions.exp: 〜というのは：what is called / the fact is / the reason is。〜というのは has several related senses: (1) 'what is called X / the thing called X' — introducing a definition or concept; (2
questions.en: Friendship is something you can't buy with money.

questions.q: 長年の努力（　　）、夢を実現した。
questions.exp: 〜の果てに：at the very end of / after (extensive process)。〜果てに expresses that the main-clause outcome came at the end of a long, often harsh, process — 'after extensive X, finall
questions.en: After years of effort, he finally realized his dream.

questions.q: 彼が来ない（　　）、忙しいのだろう。
questions.exp: 〜ということは：what it means is / so (it follows that)。〜ということは is a discourse marker that introduces a logical conclusion from a preceding statement — 'what this means is', 'i
questions.en: His not coming means he's probably busy.

questions.q: 好き（　　）、そうでもない。
questions.exp: 〜かというと：as for whether / if you ask whether。〜かというと embeds a question for discussion — 'as for whether X, (the answer is Y)'. Often used when the speaker raises a qu
questions.en: As for whether I like it — not really.

questions.q: 本件は合意した（　　）扱います。
questions.exp: 〜ものとして：as / regarded as / assuming that。〜ものとして is used to frame something as being treated or considered as X — 'as X' or 'regarded as X'. Often in hypothetical
questions.en: We will treat this matter as agreed upon.

questions.q: 泣い（　　）感動した。
questions.exp: 〜てもいいくらい：enough to / to the extent that (you could)。〜てもいいくらい expresses that the intensity or extent of something is so high that it justifies an extreme action — 'it's so X
questions.en: I was moved enough that I could have cried.

questions.q: 準備はできた。（　　）出発するだけだ。
questions.exp: 〜あとは：the rest is / all that's left is。〜あとは expresses that after a main action is complete, only X remains to be done — 'the rest is X' / 'all that's left is X
questions.en: Preparations are done. All that's left is to depart.

questions.q: 見る（　　）、いいですよ。
questions.exp: 〜だけなら：if only / if it's just X。〜だけなら expresses 'if it's only X (then Y is OK)' — a conditional statement that limits the scope to a minimal case. Often
questions.en: If it's just looking, it's fine.

questions.q: 彼は謝ら（　　）、逆に怒り出した。
questions.exp: 〜ないのみならず：not only does not / not just failing to。〜ないのみならず is a literary concessive-additive combining the negative ない with the 'not only' のみならず — 'not only does X not ha
questions.en: Not only did he not apologize, but he even got angry.

questions.q: ルール（　　）、行動してください。
questions.exp: 〜にしたがって：in accordance with / as (X progresses)。〜にしたがって has two related senses: (1) 'in accordance with' — following rules, orders, or guidelines (noun + にしたがって); (2) '
questions.en: Please act in accordance with the rules.

questions.q: 川（　　）歩きました。
questions.exp: 〜に沿って：along / in accordance with。〜に沿って has two related senses: (1) spatially 'along' (川に沿って歩く 'walk along the river'); (2) figuratively 'in accordance wi
questions.en: I walked along the river.

questions.q: 宿題を始めた（　　）、なかなか進まない。
questions.exp: 〜ものの：although / even though (formal)。〜ものの is a formal concessive marker meaning 'although X, Y', where the main clause often expresses a limitation, unexpect
questions.en: Although I started my homework, it's not progressing much.

questions.q: 彼はまるで何も知らない（　　）話した。
questions.exp: 〜かのように：as if / as though。〜かのように compares something to a hypothetical or imagined scenario — 'as if X were the case'. It is more literary and more
questions.en: He talked as if he knew nothing at all.

questions.q: 昔に戻れる（　　）、戻りたい。
questions.exp: 〜ものなら：if (it were) possible / if I could。〜ものなら combined with a potential form expresses a hypothetical wish that the speaker considers difficult or impossible —
questions.en: If I could go back to the past, I would.

questions.q: この小説は実話（　　）書かれた。
questions.exp: 〜をもとに：based on / on the basis of。〜をもとに / 〜をもとにして expresses that something is created, judged, or produced 'based on' a source material, data, or foundati
questions.en: This novel was written based on a true story.

questions.q: この部屋は広い（　　）、明るい。
questions.exp: 〜上に：in addition to / on top of (that)。〜上に is an additive connector meaning 'in addition to X, (also) Y'. The Y clause builds on or extends the X clause in the
questions.en: This room is spacious and also bright.

questions.q: 約束した（　　）、必ず守る。
questions.exp: 〜上は：now that / since (formal, implies commitment)。〜上は (うえは) is a formal concessive / justificatory marker — 'now that X, Y must follow'. The main clause typically express
questions.en: Now that I've promised, I'll definitely keep my word.

questions.q: 引き受けた（　　）、最後までやる。
questions.exp: 〜以上は：since / now that / as long as。〜以上(は) expresses that given the premise of the 以上 clause, a certain conclusion or obligation follows — 'since X is the c
questions.en: Now that I've taken it on, I'll see it through.

questions.q: 彼は頭がいい。（　　）、性格も素晴らしい。
questions.exp: 〜しかも：moreover / what's more / on top of that。しかも is a sentence-initial conjunction meaning 'moreover' / 'what's more' — adds additional, often surprising or emphatic
questions.en: He's smart. Moreover, his personality is wonderful.

questions.q: 物価は上がる（　　）。
questions.exp: 〜ばかりだ：nothing but / only one way (to decline / increase)。〜ばかりの / 〜ばかりだ expresses that something is trending in only one direction — 'only X-ing' / 'nothing but X'. Similar to 〜一
questions.en: Prices keep only going up.

questions.q: この薬には副作用（　　）。
questions.exp: 〜の恐れがある：risk of / fear that (may happen)。〜恐れがある expresses that there is a risk or possibility of something (usually negative) happening — 'there's a risk of X'.
questions.en: This medicine has a risk of side effects.

questions.q: 彼の意見に賛成せ（　　）。
questions.exp: 〜ざるを得ない：cannot help but / have no choice but to。〜ざるを得ない is a formal / literary construction meaning 'cannot help but X' or 'have no choice but to X'. Expresses that ext
questions.en: I cannot help but agree with his opinion.

questions.q: 泣い（　　）、すぐ笑い出した。
questions.exp: 〜たかと思うと：no sooner had / just when (unexpected change)。〜かと思うと expresses that two events happen in quick succession, often with an unexpected change — 'no sooner had X than Y'
questions.en: No sooner had she cried than she started laughing.

questions.q: 痛いけど、病院に行く（　　）。
questions.exp: 〜ほどではない：not to the extent of / not as much as。〜ほどではない is a negation of 〜ほど, expressing that something is 'not to the extent' of X — downplaying a comparison. Often us
questions.en: It hurts, but not to the extent of going to the hospital.

questions.q: 出発（　　）、準備を整えた。
questions.exp: 〜にあたり：on the occasion of / at the time of (formal)。〜にあたって marks an important occasion, milestone, or starting point at which the main-clause action is taken. It is formal
questions.en: In preparation for departure, I made ready.

questions.q: 卒業（　　）、先生に感謝を伝えた。
questions.exp: 〜にあたって：on the occasion of / at the time of (formal)。〜にあたって marks an important occasion, milestone, or starting point at which the main-clause action is taken. It is formal
questions.en: On the occasion of graduation, I conveyed my thanks to my teacher.

questions.q: 発表（　　）、リハーサルを行った。
questions.exp: 〜に先立ち：prior to / in advance of。〜に先立って marks an action taken BEFORE an important upcoming event — 'prior to X, Y'. It is formal and usually appears in b
questions.en: In advance of the announcement, we held a rehearsal.

questions.q: 出発（　　）、パスポートを確認した。
questions.exp: 〜に先立って：prior to / in advance of。〜に先立って marks an action taken BEFORE an important upcoming event — 'prior to X, Y'. It is formal and usually appears in b
questions.en: Prior to departure, I checked my passport.

questions.q: 留学（　　）、彼の人生は大きく変わった。
questions.exp: 〜を契機として：taking X as the opportunity / prompted by。〜を契機に is a more formal / literary variant of 〜をきっかけに, marking an opportunity or triggering event that led to a significa
questions.en: Taking studying abroad as the turning point, his life changed greatly.

questions.q: 退職（　　）、趣味に時間を使うようになった。
questions.exp: 〜を契機に：taking X as the opportunity / prompted by。〜を契機に is a more formal / literary variant of 〜をきっかけに, marking an opportunity or triggering event that led to a significa
questions.en: Prompted by retirement, he began spending time on hobbies.

questions.q: お一人（　　）、1000円です。
questions.exp: 〜につき：due to / because of / per。〜につき has two related senses: (1) 'due to / because of' — giving a formal reason, common in signage ('closed due to renov
questions.en: It's 1000 yen per person.

questions.q: 改装中（　　）、通路が狭くなっています。
questions.exp: 〜につき：due to / because of / per。〜につき has two related senses: (1) 'due to / because of' — giving a formal reason, common in signage ('closed due to renov
questions.en: Due to renovations, the passageway has been narrowed.

questions.q: 彼は正直である（　　）、損をすることも多い。
questions.exp: 〜がゆえに：because / therefore (literary)。〜ゆえに is a literary / archaic causal marker meaning 'because X, therefore Y'. It is used in essays, formal writing, legal
questions.en: Precisely because he is honest, he often loses out.

questions.q: 足跡が残っている（　　）、犯人は2人だと分かった。
questions.exp: 〜ことから：from the fact that / based on the fact。〜ことから presents a fact or evidence as the basis for a conclusion, explanation, or designation — 'from the fact that X, (Y
questions.en: From the fact that footprints remained, we knew there were two culprits.

questions.q: 彼の顔が赤い（　　）、お酒を飲んだことが分かる。
questions.exp: 〜ことから：from the fact that / based on the fact。〜ことから presents a fact or evidence as the basis for a conclusion, explanation, or designation — 'from the fact that X, (Y
questions.en: From his red face, you can tell he's been drinking.

questions.q: 悲しみ（　　）、泣き出した。
questions.exp: 〜のあまり：out of excess / from too much。〜あまり expresses an excessive degree of emotion, sensation, or state that led to the main-clause consequence — 'out of too
questions.en: Out of too much sadness, I burst into tears.

questions.q: 健康であれ（　　）、仕事ができる。
questions.exp: 〜ばこそ：precisely because / it is only because。〜ばこそ emphasizes the X clause as the sole or essential reason for Y — 'precisely because X, Y'. Formal / literary. Often
questions.en: It is only because one is healthy that one can work.

questions.q: 努力すれ（　　）、成功が得られる。
questions.exp: 〜ばこそ：precisely because / it is only because。〜ばこそ emphasizes the X clause as the sole or essential reason for Y — 'precisely because X, Y'. Formal / literary. Often
questions.en: Only by making effort can success be obtained.

questions.q: 難しい（　　）、挑戦する価値がある。
questions.exp: 〜からこそ：precisely because / it is exactly because。〜からこそ emphasizes that the X clause is the essential and exclusive reason for Y — 'precisely because X, Y (and no other r
questions.en: Precisely because it's difficult, it's worth the challenge.

questions.q: 努力した（　　）、成功した。
questions.exp: 〜からこそ：precisely because / it is exactly because。〜からこそ emphasizes that the X clause is the essential and exclusive reason for Y — 'precisely because X, Y (and no other r
questions.en: It's precisely because I worked hard that I succeeded.

questions.q: 経済成長（　　）、都市化が進んだ。
questions.exp: 〜に伴い：accompanying / along with / as X progresses。〜に伴って expresses that one change or event accompanies another — 'along with X, Y also (happens / changes)'. Similar to 〜に
questions.en: Along with economic growth, urbanization progressed.

questions.q: 技術の進歩（　　）、生活が便利になった。
questions.exp: 〜に伴って：accompanying / along with / as X progresses。〜に伴って expresses that one change or event accompanies another — 'along with X, Y also (happens / changes)'. Similar to 〜に
questions.en: With the progress of technology, life has become more convenient.

questions.q: 状況（　　）、対応を変える必要がある。
questions.exp: 〜に応じて：depending on / in response to / in accordance with。〜に応じて expresses proportional or responsive adjustment — the main clause action or outcome varies 'depending on' or 'in a
questions.en: We need to change our response depending on the situation.

questions.q: 需要（　　）、生産量を調整する。
questions.exp: 〜に応じて：depending on / in response to / in accordance with。〜に応じて expresses proportional or responsive adjustment — the main clause action or outcome varies 'depending on' or 'in a
questions.en: We adjust production volume in response to demand.

questions.q: 期待（　　）、彼は見事に優勝した。
questions.exp: 〜にこたえて：in response to / responding to (expectations, requests)。〜にこたえて expresses that the main-clause action responds to or meets an expectation, request, demand, or cheer from the X n
questions.en: Meeting expectations, he brilliantly won the championship.

questions.q: お客様の要望（　　）、新商品を開発した。
questions.exp: 〜にこたえて：in response to / responding to (expectations, requests)。〜にこたえて expresses that the main-clause action responds to or meets an expectation, request, demand, or cheer from the X n
questions.en: Responding to customer requests, we developed a new product.

questions.q: 天気（　　）、試合は行われる。
questions.exp: 〜にかかわらず：regardless of / irrespective of。〜にかかわらず expresses that the main clause holds regardless of variation in the X factor — 'regardless of X, Y'. Often used
questions.en: Regardless of the weather, the match will be held.

questions.q: 経験のあるなし（　　）、挑戦してみてください。
questions.exp: 〜にかかわらず：regardless of / irrespective of。〜にかかわらず expresses that the main clause holds regardless of variation in the X factor — 'regardless of X, Y'. Often used
questions.en: Regardless of experience, please try.

questions.q: 努力した（　　）、結果が出なかった。
questions.exp: 〜にもかかわらず：despite / in spite of / even though。〜にもかかわらず is a formal concessive marker meaning 'despite X, Y'. More formal than のに or が, with no emotional connotation —
questions.en: Despite making an effort, no result came.

questions.q: 忙しい（　　）、手伝ってくれた。
questions.exp: 〜にもかかわらず：despite / in spite of / even though。〜にもかかわらず is a formal concessive marker meaning 'despite X, Y'. More formal than のに or が, with no emotional connotation —
questions.en: Despite being busy, he helped me.

questions.q: 国籍（　　）、みんな歓迎します。
questions.exp: 〜を問わず：regardless of / without regard to。〜を問わず expresses that a main-clause condition or rule applies regardless of variation in the X factor — 'regardless of X'
questions.en: Regardless of nationality, all are welcome.

questions.q: 経験の有無（　　）、参加可能です。
questions.exp: 〜を問わず：regardless of / without regard to。〜を問わず expresses that a main-clause condition or rule applies regardless of variation in the X factor — 'regardless of X'
questions.en: Regardless of prior experience, participation is possible.

questions.q: 冗談（　　）、話を戻しましょう。
questions.exp: 〜はともかくとして：setting X aside / putting X aside / regardless of。〜はともかく sets aside a topic or factor as not worth discussing right now, to focus on something else — 'setting X aside, le
questions.en: Joking aside, let's get back to the topic.

questions.q: それ（　　）、次の議題に移ります。
questions.exp: 〜はさておき：setting X aside (formal)。〜はさておき is a more formal variant of 〜はともかく, used to set a topic aside and move to another. Common in written Japanese, es
questions.en: Setting that aside, let's move to the next topic.

questions.q: 詳細（　　）、概要を説明します。
questions.exp: 〜はさておき：setting X aside (formal)。〜はさておき is a more formal variant of 〜はともかく, used to set a topic aside and move to another. Common in written Japanese, es
questions.en: Setting details aside, I'll explain the outline.

questions.q: うちの子（　　）、嘘はつかない。
questions.exp: 〜に限って：of all (things/people/times) / precisely when。〜に限って has two related senses: (1) 'of all X' — emphasizing that X is the exception or the one that (unexpectedly) did Y;
questions.en: Not my child — he would never lie.

questions.q: 今日（　　）、傘を忘れた。
questions.exp: 〜に限って：of all (things/people/times) / precisely when。〜に限って has two related senses: (1) 'of all X' — emphasizing that X is the exception or the one that (unexpectedly) did Y;
questions.en: Of all days, today I forgot my umbrella.

questions.q: 子供（　　）、大人もこの本を楽しめる。
questions.exp: 〜に限らず：not limited to / not only X (but also)。〜に限らず expresses that the main-clause statement applies not only to X but also to other, related cases — 'not limited to
questions.en: Not limited to children, adults can also enjoy this book.

questions.q: 女性（　　）、男性も料理を楽しむ時代だ。
questions.exp: 〜に限らず：not limited to / not only X (but also)。〜に限らず expresses that the main-clause statement applies not only to X but also to other, related cases — 'not limited to
questions.en: This is an era when not only women but men also enjoy cooking.

questions.q: 彼の優しさに、感動せ（　　）。
questions.exp: 〜ずにはいられない：can't help doing / can't stop oneself from。〜ずにはいられない is a strong involuntary expression — 'can't help but do X'. The speaker feels compelled to act due to strong e
questions.en: I can't help being moved by his kindness.

questions.q: この本を読むと、考え（　　）。
questions.exp: 〜ずにはいられない：can't help doing / can't stop oneself from。〜ずにはいられない is a strong involuntary expression — 'can't help but do X'. The speaker feels compelled to act due to strong e
questions.en: Reading this book, I can't help but think.

questions.q: 彼の危険を思うと、寝（　　）。
questions.exp: 〜てはいられない：can't afford to be (doing X) / can't stay (in state X)。〜てはいられない expresses that the speaker cannot afford to remain in a state or continue doing an action due to urgency or pre
questions.en: Thinking of his danger, I can't just sleep.

questions.q: 失敗する（　　）、挑戦するべきだ。
questions.exp: 〜にせよ：even if / whether or not (formal)。〜にしろ / 〜にせよ are formal literary variants of 〜にしても ('even if'). They can appear singly as a concessive, or in paired form
questions.en: Even if you fail, you should try.

questions.q: どちら（　　）、結論は変わらない。
questions.exp: 〜にしろ：even if / whether or not (formal)。〜にしろ / 〜にせよ are formal literary variants of 〜にしても ('even if'). They can appear singly as a concessive, or in paired form
questions.en: Either way, the conclusion won't change.

questions.q: 行く（　　）、明日の朝だ。
questions.exp: 〜としても：even if / even assuming / even as。〜としても is a concessive marker meaning 'even if X', 'even assuming X'. It hypothetically accepts X and then states somethi
questions.en: Even if I go, it will be tomorrow morning.

questions.q: 嘘だ（　　）、信じたい。
questions.exp: 〜としても：even if / even assuming / even as。〜としても is a concessive marker meaning 'even if X', 'even assuming X'. It hypothetically accepts X and then states somethi
questions.en: Even if it's a lie, I want to believe it.

questions.q: 健康を取り戻す（　　）、運動を始めた。
questions.exp: 〜べく：in order to / for the purpose of (literary)。〜べく is a literary purpose marker meaning 'in order to X'. It is derived from the classical form that also gives 〜べきだ ('s
questions.en: In order to regain my health, I started exercising.

questions.q: 事件を解決す（　　）、警察は調査を続けた。
questions.exp: 〜べく：in order to / for the purpose of (literary)。〜べく is a literary purpose marker meaning 'in order to X'. It is derived from the classical form that also gives 〜べきだ ('s
questions.en: In order to solve the case, the police continued their investigation.

questions.q: 子供（　　）、大人も楽しめる。
questions.exp: 〜はもちろんのこと：not to mention / of course / needless to say。〜はもちろん presents X as an expected or given case and the main clause adds an additional, sometimes more surprising, case —
questions.en: Needless to say children, but adults can also enjoy it.

questions.q: 一年（　　）観光客が多い。
questions.exp: 〜を通じて：through / via / throughout (all of)。〜を通じて / 〜を通して has two related senses: (1) 'through / via' — by means of something (intermediary, medium, experience); (2
questions.en: There are many tourists throughout the year.

questions.q: この経験（　　）、多くのことを学んだ。
questions.exp: 〜を通して：through / via / throughout (all of)。〜を通じて / 〜を通して has two related senses: (1) 'through / via' — by means of something (intermediary, medium, experience); (2
questions.en: Through this experience, I learned many things.

questions.q: 長年（　　）努力が実を結んだ。
questions.exp: 〜にわたる：over / spanning / across (a period or extent)。〜にわたって expresses that the main-clause action or state spans a period, area, or extent — 'over / throughout X'. Formal re
questions.en: Many years of effort bore fruit.

questions.q: 広範囲（　　）雨が降った。
questions.exp: 〜にわたり：over / spanning / across (a period or extent)。〜にわたって expresses that the main-clause action or state spans a period, area, or extent — 'over / throughout X'. Formal re
questions.en: It rained over a wide area.

questions.q: 感謝（　　）プレゼントを贈る。
questions.exp: 〜を込めて：with (feeling) / putting (feeling) into。〜を込めて expresses doing an action with a particular feeling or emotion 'put into' it — 'with love', 'with care', 'with gra
questions.en: I give a present with gratitude.

questions.q: 心（　　）歌った。
questions.exp: 〜を込めて：with (feeling) / putting (feeling) into。〜を込めて expresses doing an action with a particular feeling or emotion 'put into' it — 'with love', 'with care', 'with gra
questions.en: I sang with all my heart.

questions.q: 子供（　　）、よく知っている。
questions.exp: 〜にしては：for (a) / considering X (surprising)。〜にしては expresses that the main-clause observation is surprising considering the X premise — 'for an X, (Y is unexpected)'
questions.en: For a child, he knows a lot.

questions.q: 新人（　　）、仕事ができる。
questions.exp: 〜にしては：for (a) / considering X (surprising)。〜にしては expresses that the main-clause observation is surprising considering the X premise — 'for an X, (Y is unexpected)'
questions.en: For a newcomer, he's capable at his work.

questions.q: 今（　　）思えば、あれが最後のチャンスだった。
questions.exp: 〜にして：at (the point of) / being (X), (emphatic)。〜にして has several related literary senses: (1) at a specific (often emphatic) point — '80歳にして' ('at 80'); (2) 'being X (s
questions.en: Now that I think about it, that was my last chance.

questions.q: 彼は医者（　　）作家でもある。
questions.exp: 〜にして：at (the point of) / being (X), (emphatic)。〜にして has several related literary senses: (1) at a specific (often emphatic) point — '80歳にして' ('at 80'); (2) 'being X (s
questions.en: He is a doctor and also a writer.

questions.q: 高い（　　）。本当に美味しい。
questions.exp: 〜だけのことはある：(it's) as expected from / lives up to。〜だけのことはある expresses that the main-clause praise or quality is fitting given a stated premise — 'being X, it's no surpris
questions.en: It lives up to its high price — really delicious.

questions.q: プロ（　　）ね。
questions.exp: 〜だけのことはある：(it's) as expected from / lives up to。〜だけのことはある expresses that the main-clause praise or quality is fitting given a stated premise — 'being X, it's no surpris
questions.en: He really lives up to being a pro.

questions.q: そんなことで病院に行く（　　）。
questions.exp: 〜までもない：no need to even / not worth (doing)。〜までもない expresses that an action is so unnecessary or obvious that it isn't even worth doing — 'no need to go so far as X
questions.en: There's no need to go so far as to visit a hospital for that.

questions.q: わざわざ行く（　　）よ。
questions.exp: 〜までもない：no need to even / not worth (doing)。〜までもない expresses that an action is so unnecessary or obvious that it isn't even worth doing — 'no need to go so far as X
questions.en: No need to go out of your way.

questions.q: それが現実（　　）。
questions.exp: 〜というものだ：that's what X is / that's (the nature of) X。〜というものだ expresses a speaker's evaluative conclusion or universal statement about what something is or should be — 'that'
questions.en: That's what reality is.

questions.q: 苦労して手に入れたものほど大切にする、（　　）。
questions.exp: 〜というものだ：that's what X is / that's (the nature of) X。〜というものだ expresses a speaker's evaluative conclusion or universal statement about what something is or should be — 'that'
questions.en: The harder something is to obtain, the more you treasure it — that's how it is.

questions.q: 最近忙しい（　　）、連絡が遅くなりました。
questions.exp: 〜もので：because / it's because (excusing / justifying)。〜ものだから gives a reason with an excuse-making or justifying flavor — 'it's because X (so please understand)'. Often used w
questions.en: I've been busy lately, so my reply was delayed.

questions.q: 小さい子供がいる（　　）すから、夜は外出できません。
questions.exp: 〜もので：because / it's because (excusing / justifying)。〜ものだから gives a reason with an excuse-making or justifying flavor — 'it's because X (so please understand)'. Often used w
questions.en: Since I have small children, I can't go out at night.

questions.q: 見た目（　　）、すごく美味しそうだ。
questions.exp: 〜からして：judging from / starting with (as a representative example)。〜からして has two related senses: (1) 'judging from X' — where X is given as a basis for the main-clause judgment; (2) 'star
questions.en: Just from the look, it seems very delicious.

questions.q: 名前（　　）ユニークだ。
questions.exp: 〜からして：judging from / starting with (as a representative example)。〜からして has two related senses: (1) 'judging from X' — where X is given as a basis for the main-clause judgment; (2) 'star
questions.en: Starting with the name, it's unique.

questions.q: 彼の話し方（　　）、怒っているようだ。
questions.exp: 〜からすれば：from X's standpoint / judging from。〜からすると / 〜からすれば expresses that the main clause is judged from the perspective of X — 'from X's standpoint' or 'judging f
questions.en: Judging from how he's speaking, he seems angry.

questions.q: 外国人（　　）、日本語の漢字は難しい。
questions.exp: 〜からすると：from X's standpoint / judging from。〜からすると / 〜からすれば expresses that the main clause is judged from the perspective of X — 'from X's standpoint' or 'judging f
questions.en: From a foreigner's standpoint, Japanese kanji are difficult.

questions.q: 日本に来た（　　）、日本語をマスターしたい。
questions.exp: 〜からには：now that / since (with strong obligation)。〜からには expresses a strong commitment based on a premise — 'now that X, one must Y'. The main clause typically expresses o
questions.en: Now that I've come to Japan, I want to master Japanese.

questions.q: やる（　　）、最後までやる。
questions.exp: 〜からには：now that / since (with strong obligation)。〜からには expresses a strong commitment based on a premise — 'now that X, one must Y'. The main clause typically expresses o
questions.en: If I'm going to do it, I'll see it through.

questions.q: この問題は日本（　　）、世界中で議論されている。
questions.exp: 〜のみならず：not only X but also Y (literary)。〜のみならず is a literary 'not only' construction — the most formal variant of the 'not only' family (〜だけでなく, 〜ばかりでなく, 〜のみならず
questions.en: This issue is debated not only in Japan but around the world.

questions.q: 学生（　　）、大人にも役立つ本だ。
questions.exp: 〜のみならず：not only X but also Y (literary)。〜のみならず is a literary 'not only' construction — the most formal variant of the 'not only' family (〜だけでなく, 〜ばかりでなく, 〜のみならず
questions.en: This book is useful not only for students but also for adults.

questions.q: 忙しい（　　）、家族の時間は大切だ。
questions.exp: 〜とはいえ：although / that said / even so。〜とはいえ is a formal concessive meaning 'although X' or 'that said, X'. It hedges a statement by noting that while X is tru
questions.en: Busy as I am, family time is important.

questions.q: 子供（　　）、責任感がある。
questions.exp: 〜とはいえ：although / that said / even so。〜とはいえ is a formal concessive meaning 'although X' or 'that said, X'. It hedges a statement by noting that while X is tru
questions.en: Although he's a child, he has a sense of responsibility.

questions.q: お金が大事だ（　　）、それがすべてではない。
questions.exp: 〜とはいうものの：that said / although one says。〜とはいうものの is a literary concessive similar to 〜とはいえ — 'although one says X, (the reality is Y)'. Often used to qualify a
questions.en: Although we say money is important, it's not everything.

questions.q: 便利だ（　　）、問題もある。
questions.exp: 〜とはいうものの：that said / although one says。〜とはいうものの is a literary concessive similar to 〜とはいえ — 'although one says X, (the reality is Y)'. Often used to qualify a
questions.en: Although it's called convenient, it also has problems.

questions.q: 私（　　）、そんなのは大した問題じゃない。
questions.exp: 〜にしてみれば：from X's perspective / from X's point of view。〜にしてみれば / 〜にしてみたら expresses the main-clause observation from the perspective of a specific person or group — 'from X's p
questions.en: From my perspective, that's not a big deal.

questions.q: 外国人（　　）、難しいかもしれない。
questions.exp: 〜にしてみたら：from X's perspective / from X's point of view。〜にしてみれば / 〜にしてみたら expresses the main-clause observation from the perspective of a specific person or group — 'from X's p
questions.en: From a foreigner's perspective, it may be difficult.

questions.q: 若い（　　）、何でも許されるわけではない。
questions.exp: 〜からといって：just because / even though (one says)。〜からといって rejects a simplistic causal inference — 'just because X (doesn't mean Y)'. The main clause often uses a negative
questions.en: Just because you're young doesn't mean everything is forgiven.

questions.q: 高い（　　）、必ずしもいいとは限らない。
questions.exp: 〜からといって：just because / even though (one says)。〜からといって rejects a simplistic causal inference — 'just because X (doesn't mean Y)'. The main clause often uses a negative
questions.en: Just because it's expensive doesn't mean it's necessarily good.

questions.q: 真面目な彼（　　）、ちゃんと宿題をしているはずだ。
questions.exp: 〜のことだから：knowing X / given that it's X。〜のことだから expresses that the speaker is making a prediction based on knowledge of X's character or habits — 'knowing X, (t
questions.en: Knowing he's so diligent, he must be doing his homework properly.

questions.q: 忘れっぽい私（　　）、また鍵を忘れるかもしれない。
questions.exp: 〜のことだから：knowing X / given that it's X。〜のことだから expresses that the speaker is making a prediction based on knowledge of X's character or habits — 'knowing X, (t
questions.en: Knowing forgetful me, I might forget my keys again.

questions.q: 子供が生まれ（　　）、自由な時間が全くない。
questions.exp: 〜てからというもの：ever since / since that time。〜てからというもの emphasizes a dramatic change or ongoing state that began from a specific past event — 'ever since X, Y has con
questions.en: Ever since my child was born, I have had no free time at all.

questions.q: 給料は低いが、仕事がある（　　）。
questions.exp: 〜だけましだ：at least X (is better than nothing) / could be worse。〜だけましだ expresses a minor consolation — 'at least X is the case, and that's better than worse alternatives'. Used when fi
questions.en: My salary is low, but at least I have a job.

questions.q: 風邪は辛いが、熱がない（　　）。
questions.exp: 〜だけましだ：at least X (is better than nothing) / could be worse。〜だけましだ expresses a minor consolation — 'at least X is the case, and that's better than worse alternatives'. Used when fi
questions.en: Having a cold is rough, but at least I don't have a fever.

questions.q: ドアを開ける（　　）、猫が飛び出した。
questions.exp: 〜や否や：as soon as / the moment that (literary)。〜や否や is a literary / formal construction meaning 'as soon as X, Y'. The main clause event follows immediately and withou
questions.en: The moment I opened the door, the cat jumped out.

questions.q: 始業のベルが鳴る（　　）、生徒たちは席についた。
questions.exp: 〜や否や：as soon as / the moment that (literary)。〜や否や is a literary / formal construction meaning 'as soon as X, Y'. The main clause event follows immediately and withou
questions.en: The moment the opening bell rang, the students took their seats.

questions.q: 片付け（　　）、子供が散らかす。
questions.exp: 〜たそばから：as soon as / immediately after (with repeated undoing)。〜そばから expresses 'no sooner had X happened than Y undid it' — a repetitive cycle where an action is immediately negated o
questions.en: As soon as I clean up, the children make a mess.

questions.q: 初めて（　　）、もう慣れたでしょう。
questions.exp: 〜じゃあるまいし：it's not as if / since it's not like。〜じゃあるまいし expresses 'it's not as if X, so (you should act accordingly)' — a construction that rejects a childish or unrea
questions.en: It's not your first time — surely you're used to it by now.

questions.q: プロ（　　）、そんなに完璧を求めるな。
questions.exp: 〜じゃあるまいし：it's not as if / since it's not like。〜じゃあるまいし expresses 'it's not as if X, so (you should act accordingly)' — a construction that rejects a childish or unrea
questions.en: You're not a pro, so don't demand such perfection.

questions.q: 子供は「早く行こう」（　　）、私の手を引っ張った。
questions.exp: 〜とばかりに：as if to say / seemingly saying。〜とばかりに expresses that someone's action or behavior seems to convey a specific message, without them actually saying it —
questions.en: The child pulled my hand as if to say 'let's go quickly'.

questions.q: 彼女は「知らない」（　　）、顔を背けた。
questions.exp: 〜とばかりに：as if to say / seemingly saying。〜とばかりに expresses that someone's action or behavior seems to convey a specific message, without them actually saying it —
questions.en: She turned her face away as if to say 'I don't know'.

questions.q: 緊急（　　）、すぐ駆けつけます。
questions.exp: 〜とあれば：if it's (a case of) / if it's for。〜とあれば expresses a hypothetical or conditional framing — 'if it is the case of X' / 'if it's for X'. Often carries a sens
questions.en: If it's an emergency, I'll rush over immediately.

questions.q: 必要（　　）、お手伝いします。
questions.exp: 〜とあれば：if it's (a case of) / if it's for。〜とあれば expresses a hypothetical or conditional framing — 'if it is the case of X' / 'if it's for X'. Often carries a sens
questions.en: If it's necessary, I'll help.

questions.q: 毎日とは言わ（　　）、週に一度は運動したい。
questions.exp: 〜ないまでも：even if not quite X / not necessarily X, but at least。〜ないまでも expresses 'even if not quite X, at least Y' — the speaker concedes that the full X is not achieved, but Y, a less
questions.en: Not every day, but at least once a week I'd like to exercise.

questions.q: 直接会わ（　　）、電話くらいはしてほしい。
questions.exp: 〜ないまでも：even if not quite X / not necessarily X, but at least。〜ないまでも expresses 'even if not quite X, at least Y' — the speaker concedes that the full X is not achieved, but Y, a less
questions.en: Even if you don't meet in person, I'd at least like you to call.

questions.q: 青空（　　）、ピクニックを楽しんだ。
questions.exp: 〜のもとで：under (the guidance / condition / auspices of)。〜のもとで / 〜のもとに expresses that an action takes place under the supervision, guidance, conditions, or influence of X — 'und
questions.en: We enjoyed a picnic under the blue sky.

questions.q: 厳しい条件（　　）、契約が結ばれた。
questions.exp: 〜のもとに：under (the guidance / condition / auspices of)。〜のもとで / 〜のもとに expresses that an action takes place under the supervision, guidance, conditions, or influence of X — 'und
questions.en: The contract was concluded under strict conditions.

questions.q: 今日は終わり（　　）。
questions.exp: 〜そうもない：doesn't look like / there's no sign of。〜そうにない / 〜そうもない is the negative of the appearance-based そうだ — 'doesn't look like X will happen'. Common with future pred

questions.q: 言いたいことも言え（　　）だった。
questions.exp: 〜ずじまい：ended up not (doing) / didn't manage to。〜ずじまい expresses regret that an intended action was not carried out — 'ended up not X-ing'. Common in retrospectives: 'I
questions.en: I ended up not saying what I wanted to say.

questions.q: 結局、本を読ま（　　）になった。
questions.exp: 〜ずじまい：ended up not (doing) / didn't manage to。〜ずじまい expresses regret that an intended action was not carried out — 'ended up not X-ing'. Common in retrospectives: 'I
questions.en: In the end, I didn't manage to read the book.

questions.q: 走っ（　　）、もう間に合わない。
questions.exp: 〜たところで：even if / no matter if (futile effort)。〜たところで expresses that even if X is done, the main-clause outcome will not change — 'even if you do X, (Y won't change /
questions.en: Even if I run, I won't make it in time.

questions.q: 言っ（　　）、どうにもならない。
questions.exp: 〜たところで：even if / no matter if (futile effort)。〜たところで expresses that even if X is done, the main-clause outcome will not change — 'even if you do X, (Y won't change /
questions.en: Even if I say it, nothing will change.

questions.q: （　　）初めてのことなので、不安だ。
questions.exp: 〜なにしろ：after all / you see / it's because。なにしろ is a sentence-initial adverb that introduces an emphatic explanation or justification — 'after all', 'the thing is'
questions.en: You see, it's my first time, so I'm nervous.

questions.q: （　　）忙しかったから、連絡できなかった。
questions.exp: 〜なにしろ：after all / you see / it's because。なにしろ is a sentence-initial adverb that introduces an emphatic explanation or justification — 'after all', 'the thing is'
questions.en: The thing is, I was so busy I couldn't contact you.

questions.q: （　　）忙しいので、時間がない。
questions.exp: 〜なにせ：after all / the thing is。なにせ is a sentence-initial adverb very similar in function to なにしろ — introducing an emphatic explanation. 'After all X' o
questions.en: The thing is, I'm busy, so I don't have time.

questions.q: （　　）初めてなので、ドキドキします。
questions.exp: 〜なにせ：after all / the thing is。なにせ is a sentence-initial adverb very similar in function to なにしろ — introducing an emphatic explanation. 'After all X' o
questions.en: Since it's my first time, I'm nervous.

questions.q: 便利な（　　）、危険もある。
questions.exp: 〜反面：on the other hand / while (one side)。〜反面 presents two contrasting aspects of the same subject — 'on the one hand X, on the other Y'. Functionally similar to
questions.en: It's convenient, but on the other hand, there's also danger.

questions.q: 彼は優しい（　　）、厳しい面もある。
questions.exp: 〜反面：on the other hand / while (one side)。〜反面 presents two contrasting aspects of the same subject — 'on the one hand X, on the other Y'. Functionally similar to
questions.en: He is kind, yet also has a strict side.

questions.q: 子供（　　）、両親は絶対的な存在だ。
questions.exp: 〜から見れば：from the viewpoint of / from X's perspective。〜から見ると expresses that an evaluation or observation is made from a specific vantage point — 'from X's viewpoint'. Very cl
questions.en: From a child's perspective, parents are absolute figures.

questions.q: データ（　　）、この商品は人気がある。
questions.exp: 〜から見て：from the viewpoint of / from X's perspective。〜から見ると expresses that an evaluation or observation is made from a specific vantage point — 'from X's viewpoint'. Very cl
questions.en: Judging from the data, this product is popular.

questions.q: つまり、彼は来ない（　　）。
questions.exp: 〜ということだ：I hear that / it means that。〜ということだ has two related senses: (1) hearsay / report — 'I hear that X' / 'they say X'; (2) meaning / conclusion — 'it me
questions.en: In other words, it means he isn't coming.

questions.q: 議論（　　）、結論が出た。
questions.exp: 〜の結果：as a result of / after。〜結果 (けっか) introduces the result or outcome of a process — 'as a result of X, Y'. Formal / written register, common in re
questions.en: As a result of the discussion, a conclusion was reached.

questions.q: 努力（　　）、成功を収めた。
questions.exp: 〜の結果：as a result of / after。〜結果 (けっか) introduces the result or outcome of a process — 'as a result of X, Y'. Formal / written register, common in re
questions.en: As a result of my effort, I achieved success.

questions.q: 結果（　　）、再検討も必要だ。
questions.exp: 〜次第では：depending on / according to。〜次第で expresses that an outcome or action depends on the X factor — 'depending on X, (the result varies)'. Similar in mea
questions.en: Depending on the result, reconsideration may be necessary.

questions.q: 君の努力（　　）、未来は変わる。
questions.exp: 〜次第で：depending on / according to。〜次第で expresses that an outcome or action depends on the X factor — 'depending on X, (the result varies)'. Similar in mea
questions.en: Depending on your effort, the future will change.

questions.q: 事故（　　）原因を調査した。
questions.exp: 〜に至る：reach / arrive at / come to。〜に至る expresses that a process, situation, or movement has reached or extended to X — 'came to X' / 'reached X'. Formal /
questions.en: We investigated the causes leading to the accident.

questions.q: 細部（　　）、丁寧に作られている。
questions.exp: 〜に至るまで：even to / all the way to / as far as。〜に至るまで expresses extension or comprehensiveness — 'even reaching X' / 'all the way to X'. Often used to emphasize the th
questions.en: It's carefully made all the way down to the details.

questions.q: 彼は服装から話し方（　　）、完璧だ。
questions.exp: 〜に至るまで：even to / all the way to / as far as。〜に至るまで expresses extension or comprehensiveness — 'even reaching X' / 'all the way to X'. Often used to emphasize the th
questions.en: He's perfect, from his clothing all the way to his way of speaking.

questions.q: ご利用（　　）は、注意事項をお読みください。
questions.exp: 〜の際に：on the occasion of / at the time of (formal)。〜際(に) marks a significant occasion or moment when something is done — 'on the occasion of X, Y'. More formal than 〜とき an
questions.en: Upon use, please read the precautions.

questions.q: 出張（　　）、京都に寄った。
questions.exp: 〜の際に：on the occasion of / at the time of (formal)。〜際(に) marks a significant occasion or moment when something is done — 'on the occasion of X, Y'. More formal than 〜とき an
questions.en: During my business trip, I stopped by Kyoto.

questions.q: 多くの困難（　　）、ここまで来た。
questions.exp: 〜を経て：after going through / via / through (a process)。〜を経て marks a process or step that was gone through before reaching the main-clause outcome — 'after going through X'. Co
questions.en: After going through many hardships, I've come this far.

questions.q: 彼は大学（　　）、会社員になった。
questions.exp: 〜を経て：after going through / via / through (a process)。〜を経て marks a process or step that was gone through before reaching the main-clause outcome — 'after going through X'. Co
questions.en: After going through university, he became a company employee.

questions.q: 過去の失敗（　　）、計画を見直した。
questions.exp: 〜を踏まえて：based on / taking into account / in view of。〜を踏まえて expresses that the main-clause action or conclusion is based on or takes into account X — 'taking X into account'
questions.en: Taking past failures into account, we revised the plan.

questions.q: 周囲の反対（　　）、彼らは結婚した。
questions.exp: 〜をよそに：ignoring / in disregard of。〜をよそに expresses that the main-clause action proceeds in disregard of X — 'ignoring X', 'in spite of X'. Often carries a
questions.en: Ignoring the opposition around them, they got married.

questions.q: 人々の批判（　　）、彼は自分の道を進んだ。
questions.exp: 〜をよそに：ignoring / in disregard of。〜をよそに expresses that the main-clause action proceeds in disregard of X — 'ignoring X', 'in spite of X'. Often carries a
questions.en: Disregarding people's criticism, he went his own way.

questions.q: 先生の指示（　　）に行動してください。
questions.exp: 〜どおり：just as / according to / exactly like。〜通り / 〜どおり expresses that an action or outcome is exactly as X was described, predicted, or specified — 'just as X', 'ac
questions.en: Please act according to the teacher's instructions.

questions.q: 成績の（　　）にかかわらず、全員合格できる。
questions.exp: 〜いかん：depending on / according to (formal)。〜いかん is a formal / literary variant of 〜次第で ('depending on'). Common in business and official statements. Variants: いかんで
questions.en: Regardless of grades, everyone can pass.

questions.q: 君の頑張り（　　）、採用が決まる。
questions.exp: 〜いかんによって：depending on / according to (formal)。〜いかん is a formal / literary variant of 〜次第で ('depending on'). Common in business and official statements. Variants: いかんで
questions.en: Depending on your effort, the hiring will be decided.

questions.q: それはあり（　　）。
questions.exp: 〜得ない：is possible / can / is capable of。〜得る (うる / える) attaches to a verb's ます-stem to indicate possibility — 'can / is possible to do X'. The dictionary form 得る
questions.en: That's impossible.

questions.q: 有名な歌手が来る（　　）、会場は満員だった。
questions.exp: 〜とあって：because it's / given that it's (special situation)。〜とあって expresses that the main-clause phenomenon is occurring because of a special circumstance X — 'because it's X (a no
questions.en: Because a famous singer was coming, the venue was full.

questions.q: 初めての海外旅行（　　）、とても緊張している。
questions.exp: 〜とあって：because it's / given that it's (special situation)。〜とあって expresses that the main-clause phenomenon is occurring because of a special circumstance X — 'because it's X (a no
questions.en: Being my first trip abroad, I'm very nervous.

questions.q: 手を洗って（　　）、食事できません。

questions.q: よく考え（　　）、決められない。
questions.exp: 〜てからでないと：not until after / unless you first。〜てからでないと expresses that the main clause cannot happen until after the X action is completed — 'unless you first do X, yo

questions.q: お金が（　　）、生活ができない。
questions.exp: 〜ないことには：unless / without doing (X, can't do Y)。〜ないことには expresses that the main-clause action or outcome is impossible unless X is done first — 'unless you do X, (Y is
questions.en: Without money, I can't live.

questions.q: 試してみ（　　）、効果は分からない。
questions.exp: 〜ないことには：unless / without doing (X, can't do Y)。〜ないことには expresses that the main-clause action or outcome is impossible unless X is done first — 'unless you do X, (Y is
questions.en: Unless you try it, you won't know the effect.

questions.q: 彼は納得した（　　）。
questions.exp: 〜とみえる：it seems that / appears to。〜とみえる expresses that something appears to be the case based on observable evidence — 'it seems that X'. Similar to 〜ようだ
questions.en: He seems to have been convinced.

questions.q: 彼の話が本当だ（　　）、大変なことだ。
questions.exp: 〜とすると：supposing / assuming / if it's the case that。〜とすれば (with variants 〜とすると, 〜としたら) expresses a hypothetical assumption — 'supposing that X, then Y'. The speaker accepts

questions.exp: 〜としたら：supposing / assuming / if it's the case that。〜とすれば (with variants 〜とすると, 〜としたら) expresses a hypothetical assumption — 'supposing that X, then Y'. The speaker accepts
questions.en: Supposing you won the lottery, what would you buy?

questions.q: 海外旅行（　　）、国内旅行さえしたことがない。

questions.q: 会話（　　）、挨拶もできない。
questions.exp: 〜はおろか：let alone / not to mention。〜はおろか expresses that even a basic/less-demanding case X is impossible, let alone a more-demanding Y — 'not even X, let a

questions.q: これはほんの一例（　　）。
questions.exp: 〜にすぎない：is nothing more than / merely。〜にすぎない expresses that something is 'nothing more than X' — a dismissive or minimizing description. Common for downplayin

questions.q: 彼の話は推測（　　）。
questions.exp: 〜にすぎない：is nothing more than / merely。〜にすぎない expresses that something is 'nothing more than X' — a dismissive or minimizing description. Common for downplayin

questions.q: どんなに頑張（　　）、無理なものは無理だよ。
questions.exp: 〜ったって：even if (casual)。〜たって / 〜っ たって is a casual contraction of 〜ても ('even if'). Common in casual speech, especially when dismissing a hypothet

questions.q: 健康（　　）人生だ。
questions.exp: 〜あっての：only possible thanks to / made possible by。〜あっての expresses that the main-clause noun is only possible because of X — 'only possible thanks to X'. The X is presente

questions.q: 家族（　　）幸せだ。
questions.exp: 〜あっての：only possible thanks to / made possible by。〜あっての expresses that the main-clause noun is only possible because of X — 'only possible thanks to X'. The X is presente
questions.en: Happiness is only possible thanks to family.

questions.q: 経験（　　）判断する。
questions.exp: 〜に照らして：in light of / in accordance with。〜に照らして expresses that something is judged or evaluated 'in light of' or 'in accordance with' a standard, rule, or preced
questions.en: I'll judge based on my experience.

questions.q: 事実（　　）考えてみよう。
questions.exp: 〜に照らして：in light of / in accordance with。〜に照らして expresses that something is judged or evaluated 'in light of' or 'in accordance with' a standard, rule, or preced
questions.en: Let's think about it in light of the facts.

questions.q: 彼が来ないのは、寂しい（　　）。
questions.exp: 〜限りだ：feel extremely / the limit of (an emotion)。〜限りだ expresses that the speaker feels something to an extreme degree — 'I'm extremely X'. Attached to an emotional adjec

questions.q: 危険（　　）状況だった。
questions.exp: 〜極まる：extremely / in the extreme。〜極まりない and 〜極まる both express extreme degree — 'extremely X' — attached to an emotional or evaluative na-adjective. Liter

questions.q: 残念（　　）結果だ。
questions.exp: 〜極まりない：extremely / in the extreme。〜極まりない and 〜極まる both express extreme degree — 'extremely X' — attached to an emotional or evaluative na-adjective. Liter
questions.en: It's an extremely regrettable result.

questions.q: 急が（　　）、間に合うだろう。
questions.exp: 〜ずとも：even without / even if not。〜ずとも is a formal / literary concessive meaning 'even without doing X' or 'even if not X'. Combines the formal negative ず

questions.q: 心配せ（　　）大丈夫だ。
questions.exp: 〜ずとも：even without / even if not。〜ずとも is a formal / literary concessive meaning 'even without doing X' or 'even if not X'. Combines the formal negative ず

questions.q: 専門家は今後の見通しを楽観的（　　）。
questions.exp: 〜とみる：see as / regard as / judge to be。〜とみる expresses that the speaker considers or judges X to be the case — 'I see it as X' / 'I regard it as X'. Formal / an
questions.en: Experts see the future outlook as optimistic.

questions.q: 簡単な（　　）、実は難しい。
questions.exp: 〜ようで：seemingly X, but actually / looks X but。〜ようで expresses apparent contradiction — 'seemingly X, but actually Y'. The surface appearance X contrasts with the deepe
questions.en: It seems simple, but it's actually difficult.

questions.q: 忙しい（　　）、暇そうだ。
questions.exp: 〜ようで：seemingly X, but actually / looks X but。〜ようで expresses apparent contradiction — 'seemingly X, but actually Y'. The surface appearance X contrasts with the deepe
questions.en: He seems busy, but actually he looks free.

questions.q: 勉強（　　）すれば、合格できる。
questions.exp: 〜さえ：if only / as long as (the minimal condition is met)。〜さえ〜ば / 〜さえすれば expresses a sufficient (often minimal) condition for the main clause to hold — 'as long as X, Y' / 'if on
